ul.li: Request: Warp (32 threads) requests 4-byte floats at contiguous addresses.
Detection: Hardware coalescer identifies that all addresses fall within the same 128-byte segment.
Execution: A single 128-byte memory transaction is dispatched to DRAM.
Efficiency: 100% (All fetched bytes are utilized by threads).

div.p: Avoid Strided Access: Using A[idx * 32] would result in 32 separate 128-byte fetches, wasting 97% of bandwidth.
